---
title: "Data Science Roadmap"
description: "A self-paced, code-first course covering Python foundations through transformers, diffusion models, and time-series — 22 deep-dive Colab notebooks across four parts."
author: "Kader Mohideen"
date: "2025-09-01"
categories: [Data Science, Machine Learning, Education, Python]
image: ../banners/data-science-roadmap.jpg
---

## Summary

A code-first self-study course that walks from `print("hello")` all the way to working transformers, diffusion models, and time-series forecasters. **22 Colab notebooks** across four parts — every concept lands as runnable code first, with a short explanation of *why it works that way*.

Most of Part 3 is built around a single shared dataset (auto-mpg cars, 1970s–80s) so concepts compound module to module: load it, clean it, explore it, model it, evaluate it. The capstone deliberately switches datasets to prove the workflow generalises.

## Course outline

| Part | Track | Modules |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Python for Data Science | 01 → 05 |
| 2 | Data Visualization | 06 → 10 |
| 3 | Data Analysis & ML Foundations | 11 → 16 |
| 4 | Machine Learning & AI (deeper dive) | 17 → 22 |

By the end you'll have written ML pipelines from scratch, implemented multi-head causal attention by hand, sampled from a diffusion model, and forecast a time series with ARIMA/Prophet/LSTM — and built up a capstone notebook that's portfolio-ready as-is.

## Audience

- **Beginners** who want a structured path from "what is a variable" to a working ML model.
- **Self-taught coders** filling gaps in Pandas, NumPy, and scikit-learn.
- **Career-switchers** building a portfolio.

## Links

- 💻 [Repo: github.com/kader-xai/data-science-roadmap](https://github.com/kader-xai/data-science-roadmap)
- 🌐 [Live course site](https://kader-xai.github.io/data-science-roadmap/)
- ▶️ [Start with Module 1 in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/kader-xai/data-science-roadmap/blob/main/module_01_python_basics.ipynb)
